## [00:00 - 03:03]

Okay, so first up, let's talk about your customer moments. Now what I mean by this is really understanding what your customers are going through. This really underpins everything we do with YouTube advertising. And in the agency, we have a bit of a mantra really that is start with the user. With all of our clients, we're looking to understand their customers and what they're doing on YouTube in the first place. So we know how to talk to them and we know how to target those particular people as well and how to position the offering so they actually want to go and buy the product or the service. Now here's the thing, what most companies tend to do is they look at the products or services they have, they try and create a beautiful video ad to really display and demonstrate their value and then they try and get that video in front of as many people as possible that kind of seem would be potentially interested. And what I'm asking you to do for YouTube is to almost flip that around and start not with your products and services but instead start with the user, start with your customer. If you can understand exactly what they're doing on YouTube and why they're there in the first place, then you can start speaking to people in a way that's not salesy. You don't have to be any sort of salesman at all in fact. What you're doing instead is you're communicating. You're looking at them almost like a I to Y one to one communication and you can talk directly to them. You can drop all the salesmanship and you can just start talking to them like you would do if you were in real life. When you do that, obviously people on YouTube absolutely love it because they get that genuine side of you. Now what I'm going to do is walk you through and make you understand why people go to YouTube and what they're doing on YouTube so you can start creating ads that will really resonate with them and make them want to buy your programs and your products and your services as opposed to you selling your programs, your products and services. Alright so let's first of all talk about the four reasons why people might go to YouTube in the first place. Now you might go to YouTube and let's take you for an example for now. You might go there because you want to know something. You're looking to learn something and so you go to YouTube, you look up information and you're hoping to watch some sort of educational video. Maybe you go to YouTube because you want to know how to do something. You go through this moment in time where you're thinking, do you know what, I really need to know how to do this. I know a couple of people I've been speaking to recently at Christmas, their boiler broke down and they went to YouTube and they fixed it by watching a YouTube video. Now we've all had that scenario or similar scenarios to that and watching a video and seeing how to do things. I know that we use lots of different types of software and tools etc in the business and we often go to YouTube to see tutorials about how to use these different tools that we might be using in the business. Likewise, you might go to YouTube because you're looking to buy something and I can't tell you the number of videos that are out there about unboxing videos. This is people on YouTube unboxing products on video and these videos go viral. It's crazy. But the thing is with these videos, there's so many other videos out there about people

## [06:06 - 09:08]

Going back in time and really thinking about how human beings actually interact with each other. Now, if we break these down, you're going to have different types of people. So people that are in the I want to know I want to do mentality are people that are actually doing their research and reviewing products. The people that are in that buying mode, looking at review videos, these people are ready to buy, but I just need that certainty. Maybe I just need to do a few last minute comparisons between different models. And then some people are going to be, I'm interested in this stuff. I'm not necessarily ready to buy, but I'm watching Gary Vaynerchuk's videos. I'm watching Seth Godin's videos because I'm just generally interested in marketing and I love those guys. So it's kind of like a different types of audiences mean different things. Now these three different mindsets, fairly enough offline, work in a way that used to be just how we used to interact when it came to being a physical bricks and mortar store. So if we bring these three mindsets together, you would have other considerations you might think about as well, things like gender, location, device, time of day, etc. that you can all use by targeting. But really what we're looking at is to say, if you're interested in this information and potentially what you have to offer, those people are kind of like your window shoppers. So I'm interested in Gary Vaynerchuk. I'm interested in Seth Godin. But when I go to YouTube, I'm kind of just watching their videos showing people that I'm interested in entrepreneurship and interested in starting a business or running a business or being more productive in a business and how to promote a business perhaps. Because I'm following those people that would always display that type of behavior. So I would be considered a window shopper for a lot of different types of businesses that would look to sell to me. That's exactly what we do do. Okay, so you've got the window shoppers there. You also have in-store shoppers. ## [09:08 - 09:34]

of audiences. But they're all on a timeline because they are going to be making decisions and moving closer and closer to making that decision of buying from either you or one of your competitors.
